program.name: Early Head Start
program.description:
Early Head Start provides free early learning and development services for infants, toddlers, and pregnant women. The program offers child care, health screenings, meals, parent education, and support services to help young children grow and learn. Services are provided through local programs in your community.

Families with children from birth to age 3 can qualify if their income is at or below the federal poverty level. Pregnant women are also eligible for prenatal services. Families receiving TANF or SSI, children in foster care, and homeless families may qualify regardless of income. Priority is given to families with the lowest income and children with disabilities.
program.description_short: Free early learning and care for infants and toddlers
program.estimated_value: Varies by program and services provided
program.website_description: Free early learning, child care, and family support services for infants, toddlers, and pregnant women

documents.text: Proof of home address (ex: lease, utility bill)
Proof of household income (ex: pay stubs, tax returns, benefit statements)
Social Security Number or proof of application for SSN
Child's birth certificate or proof of age
Child's immunization records and health information
